tool_args.label: 34th Street Transitway
tool_args.raw_text: 34th Street Transitway. Community Advisory Committee Meeting #2 Summary.

payload.description: Proposed transitway along 34th Street in Manhattan. The meeting covered transit operations, traffic operations, curbside access/deliveries, and three design options for accommodating non-bus traffic flow (eastbound only, westbound only, or outbound only).

evidence_refs.source_quote: Eric Beaton, Project Manager of the 34th Street Transitway at DOT

tool_args.raw_text: 34th Street Transitway

payload.description: Corridor along 34th Street in Manhattan, proposed location for the 34th Street Transitway

tool_args.label: Roundtable discussion topics
tool_args.raw_text: Attendees were asked to break into groups of eight to discuss three general topics that could affect the overall Transitway design: 1) transit operations , 2) traffic operations , and 3) curbside access/deliveries .

payload.claim_text: CAC attendees discussed three general topics affecting the Transitway design: transit operations, traffic operations, and curbside access/deliveries.
payload.subject: 34th Street Transitway design
payload.description: Three roundtable discussion topics at CAC Meeting #2

evidence_refs.source_quote: Attendees were asked to break into groups of eight to discuss three general topics that could affect the overall Transitway design: 1) transit operations , 2) traffic operations , and 3) curbside access/deliveries .

tool_args.label: Traffic congestion and safety concerns
tool_args.raw_text: For all three options of how the proposed Transitway would accommodate non-bus traffic flow – eastbound only, westbound only, or outbound only – CAC members voiced concern about potential traffic congestion and safety conditions on streets north and south of 34th Street.

payload.claim_text: CAC members voiced concern about potential traffic congestion and safety conditions on streets north and south of 34th Street for all three Transitway design options (eastbound only, westbound only, or outbound only).
payload.subject: 34th Street Transitway traffic impacts
payload.description: Concerns about traffic diversion impacts

evidence_refs.source_quote: For all three options of how the proposed Transitway would accommodate non-bus traffic flow – eastbound only, westbound only, or outbound only – CAC members voiced concern about potential traffic congestion and safety conditions on streets north and south of 34th Street.

tool_args.label: Safety implications of traffic diversions
tool_args.raw_text: CAC members voiced concern about safety implications of traffic diversions from 34th Street, including possible impacts on the number of crashes among vehicles, as well as between vehicles and both bicycles and pedestrians.

payload.claim_text: CAC members voiced concern about safety implications of traffic diversions from 34th Street, including possible impacts on the number of crashes among vehicles, as well as between vehicles and both bicycles and pedestrians.
payload.subject: 34th Street Transitway safety impacts
payload.description: Concerns about crash impacts from traffic diversions

evidence_refs.source_quote: CAC members voiced concern about safety implications of traffic diversions from 34th Street, including possible impacts on the number of crashes among vehicles, as well as between vehicles and both bicycles and pedestrians.

tool_args.label: Disabled community accessibility concerns
tool_args.raw_text: Another concern raised was the effect of Transitway design and operations on the disabled community, particularly the blind when crossing at intersections and wheelchair bound riders who need to access center island bus stops.

payload.claim_text: Concern raised about the effect of Transitway design and operations on the disabled community, particularly the blind when crossing at intersections and wheelchair bound riders who need to access center island bus stops.
payload.subject: 34th Street Transitway accessibility
payload.description: Accessibility concerns for disabled community

evidence_refs.source_quote: Another concern raised was the effect of Transitway design and operations on the disabled community, particularly the blind when crossing at intersections and wheelchair bound riders who need to access center island bus stops.

tool_args.label: Suggested variations for non-bus traffic flow
tool_args.raw_text: Retaining two-way non-bus traffic operations at the far ends of 34th Street. Making non-bus traffic lanes reversible by time of day.

payload.claim_text: CAC members suggested variations to the three options for dealing with non-bus traffic flow: retaining two-way non-bus traffic operations at the far ends of 34th Street, and making non-bus traffic lanes reversible by time of day.
payload.subject: 34th Street Transitway traffic design
payload.description: Suggested design variations for non-bus traffic flow

evidence_refs.source_quote: Retaining two-way non-bus traffic operations at the far ends of 34th Street. Making non-bus traffic lanes reversible by time of day.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: South side Transitway location suggestion
tool_args.raw_text: Locating the Transitway on the south side of 34th Street would be good for businesses west of Sixth Avenue, as it would impact curb access the least.

payload.claim_text: Locating the Transitway on the south side of 34th Street would be good for businesses west of Sixth Avenue, as it would impact curb access the least.
payload.subject: 34th Street Transitway design
payload.description: Suggestion to locate Transitway on south side of 34th Street

evidence_refs.source_quote: Locating the Transitway on the south side of 34th Street would be good for businesses west of Sixth Avenue, as it would impact curb access the least.

tool_args.label: Suggested variations for non-bus traffic flow
tool_args.raw_text: • Retaining two-way non-bus traffic operations at the far ends of 34th Street. • Making non-bus traffic lanes reversible by time of day.

payload.claim_text: CAC members suggested variations to the three options for dealing with non-bus traffic flow: retaining two-way non-bus traffic operations at the far ends of 34th Street, and making non-bus traffic lanes reversible by time of day.
payload.subject: 34th Street Transitway traffic design
payload.description: Suggested design variations for non-bus traffic flow

evidence_refs.source_quote: • Retaining two-way non-bus traffic operations at the far ends of 34th Street. • Making non-bus traffic lanes reversible by time of day.

tool_args.label: Outbound flow option for bus passing
tool_args.raw_text: It was noted that the Transitway design option that restricted non-bus traffic to outbound flow (i.e., eastbound east of 5th Avenue and westbound west of 6th Avenue) would offer the most opportunity for buses to pass other buses waiting at stops

payload.claim_text: The Transitway design option that restricted non-bus traffic to outbound flow (i.e., eastbound east of 5th Avenue and westbound west of 6th Avenue) would offer the most opportunity for buses to pass other buses waiting at stops.
payload.subject: 34th Street Transitway design options
payload.description: Outbound flow option provides most bus passing opportunity

evidence_refs.source_quote: It was noted that the Transitway design option that restricted non-bus traffic to outbound flow (i.e., eastbound east of 5th Avenue and westbound west of 6th Avenue) would offer the most opportunity for buses to pass other buses waiting at stops

tool_args.raw_text: Extending the #7 subway line east under 34th Street as an alternative to the Transitway.
